post.description:
With 2FA enabled, step 1 returns an ephemeral mfa_token instead of
the pair (finish on /auth/agent/2fa/verify).

schema.title: Response Agent Login Auth Agent Login Post

post.description:
Logged-in change (bloc 1): current password verified, all refresh
tokens revoked on success. Denied under impersonation (session
lifecycle of the target).

The ONLY response ever carrying the secret (QR provisioning);
2FA stays inert until /enable confirms a first valid code.

Login step 2 — PUBLIC binding: the ephemeral mfa_token in the body
IS the credential (attempts capped, then back to step 1).

schema.title: Response Expat Login Auth Expat Login Post

post.description:
200 ALWAYS (known email, honeypot, whatever): the EMAIL differs,
never the response — no enumeration.

The single-transaction creation through the shared writer, then
AUTO-LOGIN: the response is a live token pair — welcome screen next.

get.description:
The superadmin agencies table: paginated, searchable (name/slug),
sortable (created_at|name|cases_count), with derived status, seat/case
counts, the 3 onboarding gestures, the S0/S1/S2 state and the login
heartbeat. Filters (combinable, applied in SQL BEFORE pagination):
`trial_expiring_within_days`, `onboarding_incomplete` — Eric's "who expires
soon and hasn't started". A CONSTANT number of queries, no N+1.

The Prism list order, computed: done last, priority desc, due_at
asc NULLS LAST. Completion precedence: an explicit `status` beats
`include_done=false` (Prism). No due_this_week param: the pair
due_after/due_before covers any window — the front sends the week
bounds it displays (timezone-correct on ITS side).

post.description:
Assignee defaults to the acting superadmin; an explicit assignee
must be a platform superadmin too.

Optional body: completion_message, the client-facing note carried
by the done email (verbatim, never translated) and stored for life.

get.description:
The assignable platform operators (superadmin role holders) for
the task form selector — same gate as the tasks themselves.

schema.title: Response List Platform Operators Admin Operators Get

get.description:
Google / Outlook / ICS for a scheduled task (the Prism port).
400 when the task has no scheduled_at.

post.description:
Multipart upload. Limits ALIGNED on case documents: same size cap
(413 task.attachment_too_large) and same extension whitelist (415
task.attachment_type_unsupported).

schema.title: Response List Platform Task Attachments Admin Tasks  Task Id  Attachments Get

get.description:
The documents mechanism: the private bucket is re-streamed by the
backend (no signed URL anywhere in this product).

Platform agency switcher (superadmin-only, agency.create): EVERY agency.
The one read that deliberately crosses the tenant boundary.

post.description:
Superadmin-only (agency.create). Creates the agency + its first admin
atomically; the activation email is dispatched OUT of the request via
BackgroundTasks (never blocks the response).

delete.description:
Superadmin-only HARD delete (agency.create gate). Requires the
exact agency name in `confirm_name`; refuses if live non-demo cases
exist unless `force`. Purges every agency-scoped row + storage blob;
global expat accounts and their other-agency cases are untouched.

get.description:
The activation checklist, computed live from the usage
milestones/events - no checkbox state is ever stored.

post.description: Persist the dismiss (once, no un-dismiss) and return the state.

get.description:
The referrer's view: code, posed discount, godchildren (name and
referral status only — nothing of the godchild's own life).

patch.description:
Superadmin only (agency.create gate): pose the plan, cycle,
founding terms and conversion date - manual billing stays with
Eric, the app stores the deal and derives the seat capacity.

schema.title: Response List Members Agencies Me Members Get
Response List Roles Agencies Me Roles Get
Response List External Roles Agencies Me External Roles Get

post.description:
Offboarding (never a DELETE): cuts access NOW, drops the seat, and
returns the inventory (owned cases, active responsible steps) for the
front's reassignment screen. Anti-lockout: the last agent.manage
holder cannot be deactivated, by anyone.

The symmetric gesture — with the cap re-check (same rule as
accepting an invitation: coming back consumes a slot).

schema.title: Response List External Members Agencies Me External Members Get

get.description:
The agency provider directory (case_id IS NULL). Each row derives its
nature from agent_id (NULL = no access) and reports the designated role +
the template step participations a delete would break.

schema.title: Response List Directory Contacts Agencies Me External Contacts Get

post.description:
Create a NAMED provider in the agency directory (no account). Reusable
across the agency; can DESIGNATE a login later. Duplicate name → 409.

Give an EXISTING directory contact an account: creates the invitation,
linked to the contact. The contact id is unchanged; agent_id is set on
acceptance. 409 if it already has an account or the email is taken.

Invite a NEW provider: creates the directory external_contact (name) AND
the invitation, linked. agent_id is set on acceptance.

schema.title: Response List Invitations Agencies Me Invitations Get

get.description:
The columns the cases list can render: `default` (visible in the
no-view state), `locked` (cannot be hidden), `type` (render hint).

204 (not 404) when none exists — absence is a normal state: the
agent simply hasn't customized the tab, the frontend renders the
pristine zero-filter "All".

204.description: No customized 'All' exists for this entity yet.

delete.description: Reset back to the pristine zero-filter "All". Idempotent.

parameters.name: filters

schema.description: JSON-encoded AdvancedFilters tree from the filter-bar UI. Shape: {conditions: [...], groups: [{logic, conditions: [...]}]}. AND-combined with the per-field query params.
schema.title: Filters

parameters.description: JSON-encoded AdvancedFilters tree from the filter-bar UI. Shape: {conditions: [...], groups: [{logic, conditions: [...]}]}. AND-combined with the per-field query params.

parameters.name: sort_by

schema.description: Comma-separated sortable field keys (e.g. `status,created_at`), paired 1-to-1 with `order`. Omit both for the default ordering (created_at desc, id desc).
schema.title: Sort By

parameters.description: Comma-separated sortable field keys (e.g. `status,created_at`), paired 1-to-1 with `order`. Omit both for the default ordering (created_at desc, id desc).

post.description:
Edit-actions on a selection of cases (gate case.edit). The
`action` discriminator routes to set_status / set_owner / add_tags /
remove_tags. Cross-agency ids are silently ignored; affected_ids
lets the frontend refresh and deselect.

Soft-delete a selection of cases (gate case.delete). Re-deleting
an already-deleted case is a no-op.

get.description:
Existing dossiers of this email in MY agency (creation wizard,
opt-in copy). Declared BEFORE /{case_id} so the literal path wins.
Unknown email and email known only elsewhere answer the SAME [].

schema.title: Response Prefill Source Cases Prefill Source Get

post.description: Add a FAMILY member (the principal exists with the case).

patch.description:
Edit any person's civil status. The PRINCIPAL's name stays on
expat_user (full_name/relationship ignored for it).

delete.description: Remove a FAMILY member. The principal is never deletable (422).

schema.title: Response List Notes Cases  Case Id  Notes Get
Response List Custom Fields Agencies Me Custom Fields Get

patch.description:
key and field_type are immutable — archive + recreate to change
a type.

post.description: Soft archive (the only removal). Saved values are kept.

Resurrect an archived field — it reappears in forms and its kept
JSONB values become exposed/validable again. Idempotent.

get.description:
The agent's unified "to handle" queue: steps awaiting my
validation, my late steps, client documents to review, reminders to
approve on my cases - overdue first, oldest waiting first.

The "Activite des clients" bento feed: agency-wide client
gestures, aggregated per (type, case, day), 14 sliding days, 15
items max, newest first. Demo excluded, agent gestures excluded.

schema.title: Response List Documents As Agent Cases  Case Id  Documents Get
Response List Documents As Expat Expat Cases  Case Id  Documents Get
Response List Comments As Agent Cases  Case Id  Steps  Progress Id  Comments Get
Response List Comments As Expat Expat Cases  Case Id  Steps  Progress Id  Comments Get

get.description:
The CANONICAL Nidria text for a document type, tokens resolved for
the calling agency — what its clients see by default. An agency with
its own terms still gets the canonical text here: this is a preview of
the DEFAULT, not of its own version. Unknown type or no active document
→ 404 consent.document_not_found.

`type` is a plain str, not the enum, so an unknown value answers the
named 404 of the contract rather than FastAPI's generic 422.

Active documents the agent still has to accept (empty for any
agent that is not the agency admin).

schema.title: Response Agent Pending Consents Agent Pending Get

get.description:
Documents still to accept, grouped PER AGENCY holding a live case
of this client (the agency is the data controller).

schema.title: Response Expat Pending Consents Expat Pending Get

get.description:
Provider terms still to accept, grouped per agency the provider
works for (empty for a non-external agent).

schema.title: Response External Pending Consents External Pending Get
Response List My Cases External Cases Get
Response List Documents External Cases  Case Id  Documents Get

post.description:
Le livrable du prestataire (GAP-B) : depose sur l'etape DU dossier
assigne, visible par le client — deliverable par defaut.

schema.title: Response List Comments External Cases  Case Id  Steps  Progress Id  Comments Get
Response List Assignments Cases  Case Id  External Assignments Get
Response List My Cases Expat Cases Get
Response List Notifications Expat Cases  Case Id  Notifications Get

post.description:
Superadmin steps into another agency (agency.create) — issues an
impersonation token for one of its admins.

Create N dossiers from a CSV + mapping. Returns the report
immediately; invitation emails are dispatched OUT of the request via
BackgroundTasks (never N synchronous sends in the request path).

Dry-run: validate the CSV + mapping and report each row's PREDICTED
outcome WITHOUT creating any dossier or queuing any email — no
BackgroundTasks, no commit, strictly read-only.

get.description: The applicable mapping for (parcours, crm) — to pre-fill the import.

schema.title: Response List Job Runs Jobs  Job Id  Runs Get

get.description:
Shared LIBRARY samples (read-only). Distinct from GET /journeys (the
agency's own templates) — an agency consumes a sample by cloning it.

schema.title: Response List Sample Templates Journeys Library Get

post.description:
Create a journey template from the JSON the agency's own AI
produced (deterministic interpreter, zero LLM call here). Partial
import: an invalid step is rejected with its import_ai.* code while
coherent steps are created; a globally invalid JSON is a 422.
?preview=true validates and reports without writing anything.
`provider_assignments` resolves external slots: an assigned job
becomes a real participant and drops out of external_slots.

Re-generation (demande Nicolas) : REMPLACE le contenu du template
depuis un nouveau JSON — uniquement s'il n'a AUCUN dossier (actifs +
archives), garde verifiee en transaction (verrou + re-check). 409
journey.in_use sinon. Les pertes (pieces jointes du modele,
traductions) sont annoncees dans les warnings du rapport.

START an async AI translation of the EMPTY language variants
(default), or empty + STALE ones with include_stale=True (a stale
variant = AI-written, untouched by a human, whose source drifted —
it gets overwritten; human work never does). retranslate_langs is
the CONSENTED overwrite: those languages regenerate EVERY field,
human retouches included — the front confirms explicitly, the back
never infers it. Quota gated BEFORE launch; answers 202 with
translation_job_id — the agency keeps working while the front polls
/journeys/translate-jobs/{id} (one lot = one language = real
progress grain).

get.description:
The honest pre-launch number for the front's modal. `counts`
reports the per-language {empty, stale} split in both modes;
`items`/`estimated_points` follow the requested mode — with
retranslate_langs, items covers EVERY field of those languages.
Polling read: status + progress {done,total} + points_charged.

post.description:
Deep-clone a sample or own template into the calling agency. Attachments
are not cloned (the file stays on the source). The body is OPTIONAL: no
body / {} → default name "{source} (copie)"; {name} → that name.

schema.title: Response Set Canvas Layout Journeys  Template Id  Canvas Layout Put
Response Reorder Steps Journeys  Template Id  Steps Order Put
Response List Step Attachments Journeys  Template Id  Steps  Step Id  Attachments Get
Response List Step Participants Journeys  Template Id  Steps  Step Id  Participants Get
Response List Requirements Journeys  Template Id  Steps  Step Id  Requirements Get
Response Reorder Requirements Journeys  Template Id  Steps  Step Id  Requirements Order Put

get.description:
Read-only pre-delete impact: how many cases already carry a client
response for this requirement, so the front confirms strongly before
the destructive delete.

schema.title: Response List Step Case Requirements Journeys  Template Id  Steps  Step Id  Case Requirements Get
Response Reorder Step Case Requirements Journeys  Template Id  Steps  Step Id  Case Requirements Order Put
Response List Fields Journeys  Template Id  Fields Get
Response Reorder Fields Journeys  Template Id  Fields Order Put
Response List Case Fields Journeys  Template Id  Case Fields Get
Response Reorder Case Fields Journeys  Template Id  Case Fields Order Put
Response List Sections Journeys  Template Id  Sections Get
Response Reorder Sections Journeys  Template Id  Sections Order Put

patch.description:
Chaque agent regle SES notifications — l'acteur du token, personne
d'autre (aucun parametre d'identite).

get.description: Any member of the SAME agency (own avatar included).

The client's avatar, visible like their name: agencies holding a
live case only.

schema.title: Response Assign Journey Cases  Case Id  Journey Post
Response Get Timeline Cases  Case Id  Steps Get
Response List Message Templates Message Templates Get

get.description: The agency calendar view: agency-scoped, not per-case.

patch.description:
On a system role this is copy-on-write: the agency gets a custom
clone (rebinding its wearers) and the rename lands on the clone.

put.description: Copy-on-write on system roles, same as PATCH.

get.description:
Readiness probe for fly.io's HTTP healthcheck.

Actively touches the DB (single `SELECT 1`) so a frozen pool or a
dead Supabase connection surfaces as 503 instead of a 200 from a
process that can't actually serve requests. Fly removes the
machine from the LB on sustained failures and restarts it.

ActivateResponse.title: ActivateResponse
ActivateResponse.description:
Either a fresh token pair (account just activated, logged in) or
`already_active=True` with no tokens (2nd invitation of an existing
account — password untouched, go through login).

ActivityItem.title: ActivityItem
ActivityItem.description:
One AGGREGATED client gesture: (type, case, calendar day) with
its count - N same-day deposits by one client are one line. Types
are the bento vocabulary: documents_uploaded | comment_added |
account_activated | step_validated.

ActivityResponse.title: ActivityResponse
ActivityResponse.description:
GET /dashboard/activity - the agency-wide client pulse: 14
sliding days, 15 aggregated items max, newest first. Demo cases
never appear; agent gestures never appear (the agency would watch
itself).

AdminAgencyRow.title: AdminAgencyRow
AdminAgencyRow.description:
One agency row of the superadmin "Gérer les agences" table.

`status` is DERIVED from the model (no status/suspended column):
active (converted_at set, tested FIRST — it beats an unexpired trial),
trial (unconverted + future trial_ends_at, with trial_days_remaining),
expired (unconverted + past trial_ends_at), unknown (neither set — an
out-of-wizard/legacy anomaly the table exists to surface, never folded
into expired). `seats_used` = INTERNAL members (seat consumers);
`members_count` = ALL agents — the front derives externals by
subtraction. `cases_count` = live non-demo cases.

AgencyCreateRequest.title: AgencyCreateRequest
AgencyCreateRequest.description:
Superadmin-only (gated agency.create). Creates the agency + its first
admin atomically; the admin is onboarded via a set-password email.

AgencyDeleteRequest.title: AgencyDeleteRequest
AgencyDeleteRequest.description:
DELETE /agencies/{id} (superadmin, HARD delete, Groupe C). The
front makes the user type the agency name: `confirm_name` must equal
it EXACTLY (422 otherwise). `force` overrides the active-cases
guardrail (409 without it when non-demo cases exist).

AgencyDeletedResponse.title: AgencyDeletedResponse
AgencyDeletedResponse.description: The outcome of a hard deletion (also the trace's payload).

AgencySector.title: AgencySector
AgencySector.description:
The agency's business sector(s) — the SOCLE of the multi-sector
groundwork. INERT for now: nothing consumes it yet (no sector default,
no adapted vocabulary, no library filter). Extensible. Notariat lives
IN `legal` (not separate); 'création de société' is a JOURNEY, not a
sector.

AgencySubscriptionInfo.title: AgencySubscriptionInfo
AgencySubscriptionInfo.description:
Read-only settings block: the agency SEES where it stands, never
edits it - the conversion is Eric's post-closing gesture.

AgencyUpdateRequest.title: AgencyUpdateRequest
AgencyUpdateRequest.description:
`slug` is deliberately absent: immutable at MVP (public
identifier — changing it would break links and logs).

AgentNotificationPrefsPatch.title: AgentNotificationPrefsPatch
AgentNotificationPrefsPatch.description:
Chaque agent regle SES mails (audit §5). Strict : hors enum ou cle
inconnue = 422. Le critique n'apparait pas.

AgentNotificationPrefsResponse.title: AgentNotificationPrefsResponse
AgentNotificationPrefsResponse.description:
Les prefs EFFECTIVES (defauts fusionnes) — le front affiche l'etat
reel, jamais un trou.

AiUsageResponse.title: AiUsageResponse
AiUsageResponse.description:
The agency's AI monthly quota state (points). `remaining` is
served (not front-computed) — the NaN of 2026-07-05 came from the
front reading a field that did not exist.

AssignableProvider.title: AssignableProvider
AssignableProvider.description:
A provider of the agency the front can pick to fill a slot —
POLYMORPHIC: type='agent' (an is_external Agent, an ACCOUNT provider) or
type='external' (a directory external_contact with NO account, assignable
exactly the same). `id` is the agent id or the contact id per type; `role`
is the métier hint (the external role name, or the contact type).

AvailableColumn.title: AvailableColumn
AvailableColumn.description:
One row in GET /cases/columns — a column the frontend can render.
`default` = visible in the no-view state; `locked` = cannot be
hidden; `type` = frontend render hint.

BillingCycle.title: BillingCycle
BillingCycle.description:
Annual = 2 months off + concierge setup + price locked 2 years
(or as long as the subscription stays continuous). Values follow
Eric's passation vocabulary.

BulkActionResponse.title: BulkActionResponse
BulkActionResponse.description:
`examined` = ids submitted; `affected` = rows actually changed
(own-agency, mutation applied); `affected_ids` lets the frontend
refresh + deselect. examined − affected reveals ignored ids
(cross-agency, already-deleted, no-op).

BulkDeleteRequest.title: BulkDeleteRequest
BulkDeleteRequest.description:
Soft delete (case.delete). Separate route from bulk-action so the
RBAC engine gates each on its own permission (Prism splits these too).

CalendarLinkResponse.title: CalendarLinkResponse
CalendarLinkResponse.description:
The Prism calendar-link payload: Google / Outlook URLs + minimal
ICS, start/end rendered in the task's own zone.

CanvasLayoutRequest.title: CanvasLayoutRequest
CanvasLayoutRequest.description:
Replace the whole canvas layout blob (MVP-1). Keys are step ids;
foreign/stale ids are dropped server-side so the blob never rots.

CaseBillingInfo.title: CaseBillingInfo
CaseBillingInfo.description:
The dossier's billed price + margin, agent face ONLY, cost.view-gated
at the MANAGER (the block is entirely ABSENT from the payload without the
permission — not null-with-a-hint). Money as strings, never a JSON float;
`margin` is SERVED (billed − real costs), only when every real cost shares
the price's currency — otherwise null with the reason.

CaseCostsResponse.title: CaseCostsResponse
CaseCostsResponse.description:
A dossier's cost lines + its totals GROUPED BY CURRENCY (one entry per
currency present, never a cross-currency sum — converting would fabricate a
number). `default_currency` is the agency's prefill for a new line's currency
(None if the agency has not set one).

Billed price + margin (the "what is left at the end"): `margin` = billed −
Σ(real costs), SERVED, only when the price and EVERY real cost share the
same currency — otherwise null with `margin_unavailable_reason` saying why
(never a conversion, never an approximate margin).

CaseCreateRequest.title: CaseCreateRequest
CaseCreateRequest.description:
Principal-only create: family members and external contacts go
through their own endpoints.

`journey_template_id` is REQUIRED (product decision 2026-07-11): a case
without a journey is an empty shell — no steps, no timeline, nothing in
the worklist, an empty client space. "I'll assign later" produced dead
dossiers. Missing → an explicit 422. The CRM import composes this same
schema and always injects its (already mandatory) template per row —
exempt by construction; existing journey-less cases keep assign_journey.

CaseFieldCreateRequest.title: CaseFieldCreateRequest
CaseFieldCreateRequest.description:
Attach a case-level field (a client_case column, e.g. a country) to
a template's creation form. `case_field` is validated against
COLLECTABLE_CASE_FIELDS in the manager.

CaseFieldOrderRequest.title: CaseFieldOrderRequest
CaseFieldOrderRequest.description:
Full list of the template's case-field ids in the desired order
(same convention as TemplateFieldOrderRequest).

CaseFieldUpdateRequest.title: CaseFieldUpdateRequest
CaseFieldUpdateRequest.description:
Partial PATCH: required_at_creation AND/OR section move (mirrors
TemplateFieldUpdateRequest).

CaseImportRequest.title: CaseImportRequest
CaseImportRequest.description:
Exactly ONE mapping source is used, resolved in this order:
`mapping` (inline, BLOC 2) → `mapping_id` (a saved mapping) →
`crm_slug` (the saved mapping for this parcours + CRM). The inline
field keeps the BLOC 2 signature working unchanged.

CaseNoteUpdateRequest.title: CaseNoteUpdateRequest
CaseNoteUpdateRequest.description:
`is_confidential` is immutable after creation — flipping it
would silently move the note across the visibility boundary.

CaseUrgency.title: CaseUrgency
CaseUrgency.description:
Derived per-case urgency for the Dossiers list, priority-ordered (the
first that applies wins): a case is OVERDUE if any of its steps is overdue,
else TO_VALIDATE if any step awaits the agency's validation, else
AWAITING_CLIENT if its status is AWAITING_DOCUMENTS, else NEUTRAL. Same rule
as the dashboard worklist, lifted from per-step to per-case (see
src/cases/case_urgency.py). The enum order == the sort priority.

CatalogPrices.title: CatalogPrices
CatalogPrices.description:
The whole public grid, from the LIVE Paddle catalog (PADDLE_PRICE_IDS),
long-cached in memory: Paddle prices are immutable — a rotation means new
ids, a new env deploy, a fresh cache by construction.

ChangePasswordRequest.title: ChangePasswordRequest
ChangePasswordRequest.description:
Logged-in password change (bloc 1). The CURRENT password check is
what distinguishes this flow from the email reset; the new password
follows the same strength policy as the reset.

CheckoutCreateRequest.title: CheckoutCreateRequest
CheckoutCreateRequest.description:
POST /billing/checkout — the agency picks its plan and cycle; the seat
quantity is DERIVED from the real member count, never chosen here.

CheckoutCreateResponse.title: CheckoutCreateResponse
CheckoutCreateResponse.description:
What the front needs to open the hosted overlay
(Paddle.Checkout.open({transactionId})): the transaction id, and the
Paddle environment to initialise Paddle.js with.

ClientNotificationPrefsPatch.title: ClientNotificationPrefsPatch
ClientNotificationPrefsPatch.description:
Ce que l'agence regle pour SES clients (audit §5). Strict : une
valeur hors enum = 422, une cle inconnue = 422 (extra=forbid). Le
CRITIQUE (invitations, codes, acces) n'apparait pas — non reglable
par construction. progress_digest est INERTE tant que le job digest
n'existe pas (lot suivant).

CommentResponse.title: CommentResponse
CommentResponse.description:
One message in a step thread, shaped identically for both faces.
No raw `author_id` is exposed (respects the expat exclusion contract /
no internal UUID): `author_label` carries the resolved display name
and `is_mine` (computed from the authenticated identity) drives the
edit/delete affordances. `body` is null for a soft-deleted message
(the row stays so the thread keeps its order — front renders
"message supprimé").

CompleteTaskRequest.title: CompleteTaskRequest
CompleteTaskRequest.description:
Optional body of POST /complete: the client-facing note carried
by the done email (copy-paste), stored on the task.

CompletionMode.title: CompletionMode
CompletionMode.description:
How a journey step closes. `agency_validation` (default) = the
current flow: the agency closes the step. `auto` = capability to
self-complete when all concrete requirements are met — the active
trigger lands in a later wave; wave 1 only exposes the state.

SUPERSEDED by `StepValidatorType` (the "Action validée par" refonte):
`auto` ⇄ `none`, `agency_validation` ⇄ `agent`. KEPT during the
transition as a rollback-safe fallback (the migration backfills the
validator FROM it, never the reverse-loses); a later wave drops it.

ConsentDocumentType.title: ConsentDocumentType
ConsentDocumentType.description:
Legal documents subject to BLOCKING consent (point 16). The two
agency documents bind the AGENCY and are accepted once per agency
admin; the two client documents bind the client PER AGENCY (the
agency is the data controller, Nidria the processor); the external
document binds a PROVIDER for the agency whose portal they enter.

ContactChannel.title: ContactChannel
ContactChannel.description:
A client's PREFERRED contact channel — DISPLAY/preference only, NEVER
a send router. Reminders keep going out by email regardless (the phone
field is reused for phone/whatsapp; no second number). Extensible.

ContactInviteRequest.title: ContactInviteRequest
ContactInviteRequest.description:
Give an EXISTING directory contact an account. The contact id is
unchanged; agent_id is set on acceptance.

CostLineUpdateRequest.title: CostLineUpdateRequest
CostLineUpdateRequest.description:
Partial correction — a cost line is a notebook entry, editable. `currency`
lets the agency record that it paid in another money than planned.

CrmFieldOut.title: CrmFieldOut
CrmFieldOut.description: One importable CSV column of a source CRM (a mapping source).

CurrencyResponse.title: CurrencyResponse
CurrencyResponse.description:
One selectable ISO 4217 currency. The front builds its selector from
GET /currencies — never its own list (two lists always diverge). `name` is
English (the library's only locale).

CurrencyTotals.title: CurrencyTotals
CurrencyTotals.description:
The three totals FOR ONE currency — planned, real, écart — each computed
at read, never materialized, NEVER summed across currencies (no rate).

`planned_paid_in_other_currency` disambiguates the honest-but-misleading case:
a line PLANNED in this currency but PAID in another adds to `planned_total`
yet NOT to `real_total` here, so the entry can read "planned 120, real 0" as
if unpaid. This counter (lines planned here, actually paid, in another
currency) lets the front annotate — "1 line paid in another currency" —
instead of the front re-scanning every line to notice (it cannot otherwise).

CustomFieldDefinitionInline.title: CustomFieldDefinitionInline
CustomFieldDefinitionInline.description:
Active custom-field definitions, embedded in the case detail so
the frontend renders the person form in one fetch (no second call /
no cross-case cache staleness).

CustomFieldType.title: CustomFieldType
CustomFieldType.description:
Agency-defined field types — a CLOSED, minimal set. Each has a
distinct validation rule and frontend renderer. `options` applies
to SELECT / MULTI_SELECT only.

DashboardMeCounts.title: DashboardMeCounts
DashboardMeCounts.description: The four personal figures for the connected agent.

DashboardMeResponse.title: DashboardMeResponse
DashboardMeResponse.description:
Agent-centric "dashboard of action". Everything is filtered
server-side on the connected agent (responsible OR validator == me) and
the tenant — an agent sees ONLY its own actions.

DashboardResponse.title: DashboardResponse
DashboardResponse.description:
Simple counts, agency-scoped. NOTHING more — rich analytics is
V1.5 and stays there. Kept untouched (the agent-centric dashboard is
the separate /dashboard/me); a future "agency overview" may reuse it.

DashboardTodoItem.title: DashboardTodoItem
DashboardTodoItem.description:
One actionable line in the unified to-do (realize + validate mixed).
`badge` is the ROLE; `is_blocked`/`is_overdue` are state modifiers the
front composes into the visual priority (overdue > validate > realize).
Labels stay front (i18n) — the API ships keys/values, never FR text.

DeadlineCounter.title: DeadlineCounter
DeadlineCounter.description:
Resolved days-remaining counter for a step, computed backend-side
so both faces read the same number. `target_date` is the firm
`due_at` if set (source="deadline"), else started_at + estimated_days
(source="estimated"), else null (source=null → no gauge). The
green/orange/red thresholds are a FRONT decision on `days_remaining`
(negative = overdue).

DirectoryContactCreateRequest.title: DirectoryContactCreateRequest
DirectoryContactCreateRequest.description:
Create an AGENCY DIRECTORY external contact (case_id NULL): a
provider named ONCE, reusable across the agency's cases and journey
templates. NO login, NO invitation, NO seat — a named role only.
`name` is mandatory (the sole human identifier; email is nullable).

DirectoryContactListItem.title: DirectoryContactListItem
DirectoryContactListItem.description:
One directory row for the agency table. `access_state` (stable key, the
front never derives from text): 'none' (named, never invited) | 'invited'
(agent_id posed, invitation PENDING, mail sent) | 'active' (invitation
accepted, can log in). `invited_at` = when the pending invitation was
created (NULL unless 'invited'). `agent_role` names the designated
account's role; `used_in_steps` = template step participations (what a
delete would SET NULL — the agency sees what it breaks).

DocumentResponse.title: DocumentResponse
DocumentResponse.description:
Agent-facing document. `storage_path` is deliberately NOT exposed —
the path is technical, the (original) filename is the display data.
The enrichment fields (default empty so a bare `model_validate(document)`
on the single-upload responses still works) carry the aggregated-view
context: resolved step name, the requirement this doc answers (if any),
and the linked-vs-free classifier `is_requirement`.

ExpatAgencyPendingResponse.title: ExpatAgencyPendingResponse
ExpatAgencyPendingResponse.description: Pending documents of ONE agency (the expat accepts per agency).

ExpatDocumentResponse.title: ExpatDocumentResponse
ExpatDocumentResponse.description:
Client-facing document — the exclusion contract: NO internal UUID
(no uploaded_by_id). The uploader is conveyed by `uploaded_by_type`
+ `is_mine` (the front renders "Vous" / "Votre conseiller"). Same
aggregated-view context as the agent face.

ExpatNotificationResponse.title: ExpatNotificationResponse
ExpatNotificationResponse.description:
A SENT in_app reminder IS the notification (Q8 — no extra table).
Its own id stays: the V1.5 mark-as-read will need it.

ExpatParticipantResponse.title: ExpatParticipantResponse
ExpatParticipantResponse.description:
A step participant as the client sees it (responsible refonte, N).
Same anti-staffing as the responsible: 'agency' (no internal name),
'you', or 'external' with the provider's name; plus the role.

ExpatReferentResponse.title: ExpatReferentResponse
ExpatReferentResponse.description:
The named human contact at the agency (the case owner) —
the brief: « nom du référent + son mail ».

ExpatRequirementResponse.title: ExpatRequirementResponse
ExpatRequirementResponse.description:
A concrete requirement the client can see (and, for the writable
kinds, fulfill). `person_label` is the RESOLVED name so the client
knows whose passport/field is asked ("vous" is a frontend label —
the API ships the real name). Archived custom-field requirements are
filtered out upstream, never exposed.

ExpatResponsibleResponse.title: ExpatResponsibleResponse
ExpatResponsibleResponse.description:
Displayable responsible: type 'agency' (no internal agent name —
staffing doesn't face the client), 'you', or 'external' with the
contact's name. type None = not assigned yet.

ExternalAssignmentResponse.title: ExternalAssignmentResponse
ExternalAssignmentResponse.description: Agency-side view of who is assigned (the external agents).

ExternalDocumentResponse.title: ExternalDocumentResponse
ExternalDocumentResponse.description:
Provider-facing document — no internal UUID (no uploaded_by_id):
`uploaded_by_type` + `is_mine` (the provider's own uploads).

ExternalInvitationCreateRequest.title: ExternalInvitationCreateRequest
ExternalInvitationCreateRequest.description:
Invite a NEW provider: a directory external_contact (name, mandatory —
the stable label until the account exists) + an invitation (email, role).

ExternalParticipantResponse.title: ExternalParticipantResponse
ExternalParticipantResponse.description:
A step participant as the provider sees it (responsible refonte, N).
Same anti-staffing as the responsible: 'agency' (no internal name),
'you', or 'external' with the provider's name; plus the role.

ExternalPrincipalResponse.title: ExternalPrincipalResponse
ExternalPrincipalResponse.description:
The case holder's identity — the minimum a provider needs to know
WHO they are mandated for (a lawyer can't draft an act for "a client
in Paraguay"). Name only: still NO sensitive value (passport, notes…).

ExternalReferentResponse.title: ExternalReferentResponse
ExternalReferentResponse.description: The agency contact the provider can reach (the case owner).

ExternalRequirementResponse.title: ExternalRequirementResponse
ExternalRequirementResponse.description:
A requirement as the provider sees it — status, whose, what kind.
DELIBERATELY no `value`: the actual personal data (passport number,
date of birth, …) is never exposed to a third party by default.

ImpersonationTokenResponse.title: ImpersonationTokenResponse
ImpersonationTokenResponse.description:
Access token ONLY — no refresh token by design: the 30-minute
expiry IS the exit from impersonation.

ImpersonatorInfo.title: ImpersonatorInfo
ImpersonatorInfo.description:
Present on /me when the token was issued by impersonation —
the frontend banner reads it.

ImportExternalSlot.title: ImportExternalSlot
ImportExternalSlot.description:
A typed external-provider SLOT ('prestataire:<job>') to be named
by the agency: the template participant model requires a real
provider identity, so the import never invents one — the slot lists
the steps waiting for the provider of that job, plus the agency's
assignable providers (the front proposes a choice). A slot that has
a provider_assignment is resolved and no longer listed.

ImportFieldError.title: ImportFieldError
ImportFieldError.description:
A non-blocking per-cell failure: the dossier is still created, this
one field is left unset and reported.

ImportPreview.title: ImportPreview
ImportPreview.description:
The read-only dry-run: per-row predicted statuses + coerced values, with
NO dossier created, NO email queued, NO transaction. Dedup follows the SAME
agency-scoped rule as the real import — cross-agency existence is never
revealed.

ImportStepCreated.title: ImportStepCreated
ImportStepCreated.description: One step created (or, in preview, that WOULD be created).

ImportStepIgnored.title: ImportStepIgnored
ImportStepIgnored.description:
One step rejected by validation (partial import) with the exact
reason, front-renderable via the code catalog.

ImportWarningItem.title: ImportWarningItem
ImportWarningItem.description:
Non-blocking notice (ignored volet A, suspected personal data,
participants to finalize...).

JourneyCloneRequest.title: JourneyCloneRequest
JourneyCloneRequest.description: Optional new name for a deep clone; defaults to "{source} (copie)".

JourneyImportReport.title: JourneyImportReport
JourneyImportReport.description:
The import outcome, same shape in preview and creation —
`template_id` is only set when the journey was actually created.

JourneyImportRequest.title: JourneyImportRequest
JourneyImportRequest.description:
POST /journeys/import — the ROOT JSON produced by the agency's own
AI ({version, parcours}). `parcours` is validated by hand in the
import manager (never by pydantic) so every violation surfaces as an
import_ai.* catalog code with {chemin, valeur} params the front can
render in the agency's language.

`provider_assignments` (point 6 Eric) resolves external SLOTS: a map
{slot_job: {type, id}} — POLYMORPHIC: type='agent' names an is_external
Agent (account), type='external' names a directory external_contact
(no account, exactly as assignable). Optional (without it the slots stay
reporting-only). An assigned job becomes a real participant on its steps
and disappears from the preview's external_slots.

JourneySectionDetail.title: JourneySectionDetail
JourneySectionDetail.description:
A section WITH its fields, for the template detail. OPTION 1
(segmented): person fields then case fields, each in their own
position order — the response carries two lists, so the segmentation
is structural.

JourneySectionResponse.title: JourneySectionResponse
JourneySectionResponse.description:
Section METADATA (CRUD endpoints). The grouped view with fields
lives in the template detail (JourneySectionDetail).

JourneyTranslateRequest.title: JourneyTranslateRequest
JourneyTranslateRequest.description:
POST /journeys/{id}/translate — empty body = every incomplete
language. The source language is never a valid target.
`include_stale=True` also RE-translates (overwrites) the AI variants
whose source drifted; human translations and human corrections are
never touched, in any mode. `retranslate_langs` is the CONSENTED
overwrite: for those languages EVERY field of the template is
regenerated — including human work — and the hash trail is laid, so
staleness detection works afterwards on pre-feature translations.
Never implied; the front confirms explicitly before sending it.

LangTranslationCounts.title: LangTranslationCounts
LangTranslationCounts.description:
Per-language field counts: `empty` variants to fill, `stale` AI
variants whose source drifted (human work never counts here).

MemberDeactivationResponse.title: MemberDeactivationResponse
MemberDeactivationResponse.description:
POST /agencies/me/members/{agent_id}/deactivate — the INVENTORY of
what the departed agent leaves behind (nothing silent, nothing rigid:
the front chains a reassignment screen over the existing PATCHes).

MfaDisableRequest.title: MfaDisableRequest
MfaDisableRequest.description: BOTH factors required: a stolen session alone cannot disarm 2FA.

MfaEnableResponse.title: MfaEnableResponse
MfaEnableResponse.description:
The 8 one-time backup codes, IN CLEAR exactly once (bcrypt-hashed
at rest). They ARE the phone-lost recovery path.

MfaRequiredResponse.title: MfaRequiredResponse
MfaRequiredResponse.description:
Login step 1 outcome when 2FA is active: NO tokens — an ephemeral
challenge token only, consumable by /2fa/verify and rejected
everywhere else (its `type` claim is not "access").

MfaSetupResponse.title: MfaSetupResponse
MfaSetupResponse.description:
The ONLY time the secret ever leaves the server (QR provisioning);
every later response carries state booleans, never the secret.

OnboardingResponse.title: OnboardingResponse
OnboardingResponse.description:
GET /agencies/me/onboarding - the activation checklist, COMPUTED
live from the usage milestones/events (no checkbox state table: the
milestones ARE the truth). Only the dismiss is persisted.

OnboardingStepState.title: OnboardingStepState
OnboardingStepState.description:
One activation gesture: create_journey | open_case |
view_as_client.

PaymentMethodUpdateResponse.title: PaymentMethodUpdateResponse
PaymentMethodUpdateResponse.description:
POST /billing/payment-method/update — the special Paddle transaction
the front opens the overlay on (the past_due gesture).

PendingDocumentResponse.title: PendingDocumentResponse
PendingDocumentResponse.description:
An active document awaiting acceptance. `content` is content_md
with the {agency_name} token resolved; `content_hash` covers the RAW
text (what the acceptance will carry).

Permission.title: Permission
Permission.description:
The permission catalogue — the ONLY hardcoded piece of the RBAC
engine (typo-safe, autocompleted, refactorable). Synced to the
`permission` table at boot; bindings and role assignments live in
data. A permission means nothing unless a binding checks it:
adding a guarded action = one line here + one binding.

PersonCreateRequest.title: PersonCreateRequest
PersonCreateRequest.description:
Creates a FAMILY member (the PRINCIPAL exists with the case).

`email` is OPTIONAL: when given, the agency invites the member — the
member gets a read-only account (linked-or-created expat_user by email,
the same global pivot as the principal) and sees the dossier's progress
plus their OWN fields/documents. When absent, the member stays what they
are today: a person on the dossier with no login. Nothing changes for them.

PersonResponse.title: PersonResponse
PersonResponse.description:
A person on the case — PRINCIPAL or FAMILY, one homogeneous shape.
PRINCIPAL: identity (first/last/email/lang) resolved from the shared
expat_user (full_name NULL); FAMILY: carries full_name, identity
fields NULL.

PersonUpdateRequest.title: PersonUpdateRequest
PersonUpdateRequest.description:
Edits any person. For FAMILY, full_name/relationship are editable;
for PRINCIPAL they are ignored (its name lives on expat_user).
`custom_fields` is a partial MERGE keyed by definition key.

`email` (Arthur): on a person WITHOUT an account, links (or creates) the
read-only member access — exactly the creation semantics. On a person who
ALREADY has one, a different email is a 409 (remove the access, then
re-invite); empty ("" or null) or identical → clean no-op.

PlanCyclePrices.title: PlanCyclePrices
PlanCyclePrices.description:
Unit prices of ONE plan on ONE cycle — strings (decimal euros), the
costs rule everywhere. UNIT prices only: the front composes the display,
Paddle stays the sole judge of totals at payment.

PlannedCostCreateRequest.title: PlannedCostCreateRequest
PlannedCostCreateRequest.description:
A planned cost line on a template step — same shape as a real cost
(amount + label + currency). DECIMAL(18,4); the LINE's currency drives
decimals. `currency` omitted → the agency default; neither → 409.

PlannedCostUpdateRequest.title: PlannedCostUpdateRequest
PlannedCostUpdateRequest.description: Partial correction of a template planned cost.

PlatformOperatorRead.title: PlatformOperatorRead
PlatformOperatorRead.description:
One assignable platform operator (superadmin) — the task form
selector. No pagination: they are two.

PlatformTaskAttachmentRead.title: PlatformTaskAttachmentRead
PlatformTaskAttachmentRead.description: One attached file — storage_path is INTERNAL, never served.

PlatformTaskSummary.title: PlatformTaskSummary
PlatformTaskSummary.description: The sidebar badge numbers (Prism's TaskSummary, platform-wide).

PrefillSourceResponse.title: PrefillSourceResponse
PrefillSourceResponse.description:
A prefill candidate: one of the client's dossiers in MY agency
(creation wizard picker). Demo and deleted cases never appear.

PreviewCell.title: PreviewCell
PreviewCell.description:
One mapped cell of a previewed row: the COERCED value the import would
store (date normalized, country → ISO-2, …) or, when invalid, the reason.

PreviewColumn.title: PreviewColumn
PreviewColumn.description: A previewed column, in mapping order (the table's column set).

PreviewRow.title: PreviewRow
PreviewRow.description: The PREDICTED outcome of one CSV row — nothing is created.

PrincipalSummaryResponse.title: PrincipalSummaryResponse
PrincipalSummaryResponse.description:
Identity subset for list items — the full PrincipalResponse
(activated) stays a detail-endpoint concern.

ProfileUpdateRequest.title: ProfileUpdateRequest
ProfileUpdateRequest.description:
Partial PATCH of the actor's own names — both faces share the
shape. For an EXPAT, first/last_name belong to the GLOBAL identity,
shared across every agency holding a dossier: deliberate, the client
manages THEIR OWN identity (same rule as their email pivot).

ProviderRef.title: ProviderRef
ProviderRef.description:
A polymorphic provider reference in provider_assignments: an is_external
Agent (account) OR a directory external_contact (no account).

ProviderUsage.title: ProviderUsage
ProviderUsage.description:
Provider capacity (grid 2026-07), DERIVED live: `count` = external
agents (the external flow pre-creates the Agent at invitation, so this
IS actives + invitees); `included` = free tier (10 cabinet / 15 agence);
`max` = the cap (15/25, 10 on trial) — None for sur_mesure ("illimité").
Billing past the included tier is PHASE 2: nothing billed today.

ReferralDiscountState.title: ReferralDiscountState
ReferralDiscountState.description:
The POSED referral discount, read off the live sub (the spike's
simplification): percent from the discount rate, ends_at from the
sub's discount block. A discount without our referral_key (a promo
posed by hand) is NOT reported here — never dressed up as referral.

ReferralNextChange.title: ReferralNextChange
ReferralNextChange.description:
The NEXT tier drop, announced in advance — nobody discovers a
decrease in their invoice. Derived from the ledger: the first expiry
among active credits, and the tier that will remain after it
(percent=0 means the discount ends there).

ReminderCreateRequest.title: ReminderCreateRequest
ReminderCreateRequest.description:
Body source: message_template_id OR free-text message_body (at
least one). Variables are interpolated SERVER-side at creation —
the approver reads the exact final text.

ReminderStatus.title: ReminderStatus
ReminderStatus.description:
Mandatory manual approval: TO_APPROVE → APPROVED → SENT,
or CANCELLED. No send ever happens before APPROVED.

ReminderUpdateRequest.title: ReminderUpdateRequest
ReminderUpdateRequest.description:
Any edit of an APPROVED reminder sends it back to TO_APPROVE —
the approval covers exactly what goes out.

RequirementImpactResponse.title: RequirementImpactResponse
RequirementImpactResponse.description:
Impact of deleting a template requirement — read BEFORE the
destructive delete so the front can confirm strongly ("N dossiers sur
T ont deja repondu"). An instance is per targeted person, so a single
case may hold several responses: `cases_with_response` = distinct
cases with at least one PROVIDED instance, `responses_count` = the
provided instances themselves, `cases_total` = distinct cases carrying
the requirement at all (answered or not).

RequirementStateResponse.title: RequirementStateResponse
RequirementStateResponse.description:
A concrete requirement on an active step (NEW WAVE, read-only).
`status` is derived from the live person value for base/custom
fields, explicit for documents. `is_archived` flags a custom-field
whose definition the agency has archived.

RequirementValueRequest.title: RequirementValueRequest
RequirementValueRequest.description:
Client fulfillment of a base_field / custom_field requirement.
`value` is type-validated downstream against the field kind; null
clears it (requirement goes back to pending).

ResponsibleStepRef.title: ResponsibleStepRef
ResponsibleStepRef.description:
One step the deactivated agent was responsible for (active steps
only — DONE steps are history, never reassigned).

ResponsibleType.title: ResponsibleType
ResponsibleType.description:
Polymorphic responsible of a step (also used for
`journey_template_step.default_responsible_type`).

ResponsibleUpdateRequest.title: ResponsibleUpdateRequest
ResponsibleUpdateRequest.description:
Nominal responsible assignment (wave C). `responsible_type=None`
clears it. A named agent (internal OR external provider) goes in
`responsible_agent_id`; a legacy no-login contact in
`responsible_external_id`. Naming an EXTERNAL agent requires it to be
assigned to the case (wave-B coherence, enforced in the Manager).

RoleDetailResponse.title: RoleDetailResponse
RoleDetailResponse.description:
Mutation responses carry the full matrix so the Settings screen
never needs a follow-up GET. `cloned_from_role_id` set = this is
the agency's copy-on-write clone of a system role.

SavedViewDefaultAllUpdate.title: SavedViewDefaultAllUpdate
SavedViewDefaultAllUpdate.description:
Payload for PUT /views/default-all — the per-agent customizable
"All" view. A strict subset of SavedViewUpdate by design: no
`name` (server-controlled sentinel), no `is_shared`/`is_default`
(an "All" view is always private and never a named default).
`extra="forbid"` so an excluded field is a 422, not a silent drop.

SeatUsage.title: SeatUsage
SeatUsage.description:
Seat capacity, DERIVED live (grid nidria.com/#tarifs): `billed`
starts past included (3 cabinet / 6 agence) + founding offered;
`max` = 5 (cabinet), 10 (agence), 3 on trial — and None for
sur_mesure: NO cap, the front displays "illimité", never a blank.

SectionOrderRequest.title: SectionOrderRequest
SectionOrderRequest.description: Full list of the template's section ids in the desired order.

SectionUpdateRequest.title: SectionUpdateRequest
SectionUpdateRequest.description: Partial: rename and/or edit the description.

SignupAccepted.title: SignupAccepted
SignupAccepted.description:
Always the same body, email known or not (forgot-password pattern:
the EMAIL differs, never the response).

SignupRequest.title: SignupRequest
SignupRequest.description:
Stage 1: the email to verify. `website` is the HONEYPOT (hidden
field): humans leave it empty, bots fill it — non-empty = silent 200,
nothing created. `turnstile_token` is required only when the
TURNSTILE_SECRET flag is armed. extra=forbid: an unknown field (e.g.
sectors, which belong to /signup/complete) is a LOUD 422, never a
silent swallow.

SignupVerifyResponse.title: SignupVerifyResponse
SignupVerifyResponse.description:
The short-lived (30 min) completion token — long and
unbruteforcable, the only key /signup/complete accepts.

StepAttachmentResponse.title: StepAttachmentResponse
StepAttachmentResponse.description:
A file the agency attached to a template step (Feature 2). The
bytes are fetched via the dedicated download endpoint, not inlined.

StepCaseRequirementOrderRequest.title: StepCaseRequirementOrderRequest
StepCaseRequirementOrderRequest.description: Full list of the step's case-requirement ids in the desired order.

StepContentAttachment.title: StepContentAttachment
StepContentAttachment.description:
A file the agency attached to a step (Feature 2), as projected onto
the CASE timeline for all three faces. Deliberately NO `step_id`: the
faces address the download via (case_id, progress_id, attachment_id) —
the template id never leaks to the expat/external timelines (same
"names/instance-ids, never template ids" rule as blocked_by).

StepOrderRequest.title: StepOrderRequest
StepOrderRequest.description: Full list of the template's step ids in the desired order.

StepParticipantCreateRequest.title: StepParticipantCreateRequest
StepParticipantCreateRequest.description:
Add a participant on a template step. Exactly one person id, coherent
with `type`: `expat` ⟹ neither; `agent` ⟹ agent_id (or NULL = the agency
in general); `external` ⟹ external_id (a directory contact). agent_id AND
external_id together → 422 (never a raw 500). `role` cannot be `validator`.

StepParticipantResponse.title: StepParticipantResponse
StepParticipantResponse.description:
"Action à réaliser par" (N participants) projected on a case step.
Resolved upstream (name + is_external), like the responsible — the FACES
decide visibility (anti-staffing: an internal agent's name is hidden from
the expat/external timelines). `role` is a StepParticipantRole, never
`validator`.

StepParticipantRole.title: StepParticipantRole
StepParticipantRole.description:
"Action à réaliser par" — the role a participant plays on a step
(the responsible refonte: 1 responsible → N participants with roles).
CLOSED set; the `validator` role is DELIBERATELY absent — validation
stays on the untouched `validated_by_*` mechanism.

StepPrerequisitesRequest.title: StepPrerequisitesRequest
StepPrerequisitesRequest.description:
Declarative: replaces the step's full prerequisite set — the
whole template graph is re-validated on every mutation.

StepProgressUpdateRequest.title: StepProgressUpdateRequest
StepProgressUpdateRequest.description:
Step transitions + firm deadline. Responsible ASSIGNMENT moved to
its own endpoint (PUT .../responsible, gate case.edit) in wave C — this
PATCH stays the "work the steps" surface (gate step.complete). Unset
fields untouched; `due_at=null` explicitly clears the deadline.

StepRequirementOrderRequest.title: StepRequirementOrderRequest
StepRequirementOrderRequest.description:
Full list of the step's requirement ids in the desired order
(same convention as StepOrderRequest, one level down).

StepValidatorType.title: StepValidatorType
StepValidatorType.description:
"Action validée par" — who closes a journey step (symmetric to the
responsible: a TYPE at the template, the precise person at the dossier).
Reuses the responsible actor strings so both mechanisms read alike, plus
`none` = "validated by no one" = the former `auto` self-completion.

- `none`     → self-completes when all requirements are met (ex-`auto`).
- `expat`    → the case principal (client) clicks validate.
- `agent`    → the agency closes (a NAMED internal member, or — agent_id
               NULL — any member; ex-`agency_validation`, the default).
- `external` → a DESIGNATED provider (an is_external Agent, stored in
               `validated_by_agent_id` like the content-wave responsible —
               a validator must be able to log in and click, so it is an
               Agent, never a no-login external_contact).

SubscriptionCancelResponse.title: SubscriptionCancelResponse
SubscriptionCancelResponse.description:
POST /billing/subscription/cancel — cancellation at PERIOD END (the
commercial default): the date the access actually ends.

SubscriptionPlan.title: SubscriptionPlan
SubscriptionPlan.description:
Product plans (grid nidria.com/#tarifs). Included seats and caps
live in the SEATS_*/PROVIDERS_* constants (agencies_manager); the
AMOUNTS live in Paddle (PRICE_IDS env) — never in code.

SubscriptionStateResponse.title: SubscriptionStateResponse
SubscriptionStateResponse.description:
GET /billing/subscription — everything the management page shows, in
ONE response. Money as STRINGS (decimal euros), never a JSON float; the
unit prices come from the live Paddle subscription items (one cached
call), so a price rotation is reflected without a deploy.

SubscriptionUpdateRequest.title: SubscriptionUpdateRequest
SubscriptionUpdateRequest.description:
PATCH /agencies/{id}/subscription (superadmin) - the post-closing
gesture: pose the plan, cycle, founding terms and conversion date.
Partial: absent fields stay untouched.

TemplateFieldCreateRequest.title: TemplateFieldCreateRequest
TemplateFieldCreateRequest.description:
Attach a field to a template's creation form. `kind` is base_field
or custom_field (document is a requirement, not a creation field —
rejected in the manager). `section_id` lets the field be BORN ranged
(point 5: the CRM-import modal used to mass-create unsectioned
fields); None keeps the legitimate unsectioned bucket.

TemplateFieldOrderRequest.title: TemplateFieldOrderRequest
TemplateFieldOrderRequest.description:
Full list of the template's field ids in the desired order (same
convention as StepOrderRequest / StepRequirementOrderRequest).

TemplateFieldResponse.title: TemplateFieldResponse
TemplateFieldResponse.description:
A template's creation field with its RESOLVED render metadata
(label/field_type/options for a custom field, batched at read; base
fields carry none — the frontend knows the civil-status set). A
custom field whose definition was archived after attachment stays in
the list, flagged `is_archived` (mirrors requirements).

TemplateFieldUpdateRequest.title: TemplateFieldUpdateRequest
TemplateFieldUpdateRequest.description:
Partial PATCH: toggle required_at_creation AND/OR move the field to
a section. Both optional (exclude_unset distinguishes "not touched"
from "set to null" for section_id → clearing returns it to the
unsectioned bucket). Existing callers sending only required_at_creation
are unaffected.

TemplateStepCreateRequest.title: TemplateStepCreateRequest
TemplateStepCreateRequest.description:
No `position`: new steps are APPENDED; ordering is managed only
through the declarative reorder endpoint.

extra="forbid" (BUG-A, 2026-07-19): participants ("Action à réaliser
par") are a SUB-RESOURCE (/participants), not a step field — an inline
unknown key used to be silently swallowed (200, nothing written); now
it 422s loudly.

TemplateStepParticipantResponse.title: TemplateStepParticipantResponse
TemplateStepParticipantResponse.description:
A template participant ("Action à réaliser par", N). type ∈ {expat,
agent, external}. For agent/expat the editor resolves the name client-side
(member lists); for `external` the server resolves the directory contact
name (`name`), since a contact is not in the member lists.

TranslateEstimateResponse.title: TranslateEstimateResponse
TranslateEstimateResponse.description:
GET /journeys/{id}/translate/estimate — the front's honest number
(calibrated on real runs) plus the quota state. `counts` carries the
per-language {empty, stale} split in BOTH modes, so the modal can
offer the retranslate choice instead of a flat 'already complete'.

TranslationJobResponse.title: TranslationJobResponse
TranslationJobResponse.description:
An async translation job — progress.done/progress.total IS the
progress bar; poll GET /journeys/translate-jobs/{id} until
done|done_with_gaps|failed. One lot = one language.

UnsectionedFields.title: UnsectionedFields
UnsectionedFields.description:
The NULL bucket — fields not assigned to any section. Same
segmented shape, no section identity.

ValidatorUpdateRequest.title: ValidatorUpdateRequest
ValidatorUpdateRequest.description:
"Action validée par" — designate the validator on the DOSSIER
(gate case.edit), symmetric to the responsible assignment. `none`/`expat`
carry no agent; `agent` takes an optional INTERNAL member (NULL = any
member); `external` REQUIRES a provider (is_external Agent) assigned to
the case — enforced in the Manager.

WebhookAck.title: WebhookAck
WebhookAck.description:
Always 200 for a VERIFIED event (Paddle stops re-delivering); `status`
says what happened: processed | duplicate | ignored.

WorklistItem.title: WorklistItem
WorklistItem.description:
One actionable item of the unified "to handle" queue (contract
validated 2026-07-08). `type` picks the action link the front opens:
step_* carry progress_id (case timeline, step focused),
document_to_review carries document_id (validation modal),
reminder_to_approve carries reminder_id (reminders screen). A step
both awaiting my validation AND late appears ONCE as
step_to_validate with is_overdue=true (the action wins, the delay
sorts); step_overdue carries the remaining late steps I must realize.

WorklistResponse.title: WorklistResponse
WorklistResponse.description:
Sorted queue: overdue first (largest delay first), then oldest
waiting first. `counts` = per-type volumes + total.
